ddt:Target.Name: VDC_T4VS_UDS_v02
ddt:Target.Description:
History:
-------------------------------------------------- Author:Lakshmi Narayanan A Vehicle: PY1B -------------------------------------------------- v1.0 : (15-03-2019) Builder created for PY1B with the reference of XFC-Espace V project.
v2.0 : (15-03-2019) Tire circumference value added for PY1B(1952mm/195/60/R16 94T).
V3.0 (29-10-2020) HBC-SAF project applicability added
V4.0 - 16/12/2020
DID $4B97 added
------------------------------------------------------------- V02 04-05-2021
Gear box type ( AMT) Added

Data.Name: Hydraulic valves and pump.outlet EV RR
Hydraulic valves and pump.inlet EV RR
Hydraulic valves and pump.outlet EV RL
Hydraulic valves and pump.inlet EV RL
Hydraulic valves and pump.outlet EV FR
Hydraulic valves and pump.intlet EV FR
Hydraulic valves and pump.outlet EV FL
Hydraulic valves and pump.inlet EV FL
Hydraulic valves and pump.FL RR Electric shuttle EV
Hydraulic valves and pump.FR RL Electric shuttle EV
Hydraulic valves and pump.FL RR Isolating EV
Hydraulic valves and pump.FR RL Isolating EV
Hydraulic valves and pump.outlet EV RL response
Hydraulic valves and pump.inlet EV RL response
Hydraulic valves and pump.outlet EV FR response
Hydraulic valves and pump.intlet EV FR response
Hydraulic valves and pump.outlet EV FL response
Hydraulic valves and pump.inlet EV FL response
Hydraulic valves and pump.FL RR Electric shuttle EV response
Hydraulic valves and pump.FR RL Electric shuttle EV response
Hydraulic valves and pump.FL RR Isolating EV response
Hydraulic valves and pump.FR RL Isolating EV response
OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Name: IOCTL.DataIdentifier
Data.Comment: Data Identifier received in reply to InputOutputControl request

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Item.Text: CAN - TPI, reduced data, no cog + Renault dev frame

Data.Name: Brake Comfort Release function activation deactivation
Roller Bench Mode function activation deactivation
diagnosticVersion
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: HBA Threshold Decrease application data.Duration appli HBA Threshold Decrease 1
HBA Threshold Decrease application data.Duration appli HBA Threshold Decrease 2
HBA Threshold Decrease application data.Duration appli HBA Threshold Decrease 3
HBA Threshold Decrease application data.Mileage appli HBA Threshold Decrease 1
HBA Threshold Decrease application data.Mileage appli HBA Threshold Decrease 2
HBA Threshold Decrease application data.Mileage appli HBA Threshold Decrease 3
Function status.ASR function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.ASR function.inhibition by switch status
Function status.AYC function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.AYC function.inhibition by switch status
Function status.HBA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.HSA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.EBP function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.RMF function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.TSF function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.HLA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.HBB function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.ACC function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.AEBS function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.SIDIPIL function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.MULTI EXP function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.EG function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.BLA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.BHF function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.VESS function.inhibition by diag status

Item.Text: Electric shuttle valve FR/RL axe fault
Electric shuttle valve FL/RR axe fault
Missing ABS or VDC calibration parameters
Lost Communication with Add. Display on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with 4WS on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with EBA on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with ECM on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with AT on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with HEVC on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with 4WD on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with METER on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with BCM on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with MIU on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with SWAS on Brake CAN
Lost communication with USM on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with IDM on Vehicle CAN
Lost Communication with EBA on Chassis-CAN
Lost Communication with HEVC on Chassis-CAN
Lost Communication with ADAS on Chassis CAN
Lost Communication with APB on Chassis-CAN
Lost Communication with USA on Chassis-CAN
Lost Communication with IDM on Chassis-CAN
Device and failure type ODB codding
FM (Freq Mod) / PWM (Pulse Width Mod) Failures
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level / zero adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or operation obstructed or blocked
Controle Module Communication - Private CAN No transmission
Controle Module Communication - Private CAN BUS OFF
FM (Freq Mod) / PWM (Pulse Width Mod) Failures

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: CalibrationNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer specific ECU application data identification record.

The format of the application data identification structure shall be ECU specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: Activation switch state
Data.Comment: Switch to active Extended Grip function

Item.Text: Sleep or Crank fault, Disable DiagMuxDTC

Data.Name: Roller Bench Mode function inhibition state
RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Item.Text: Magnetic tone wheel control status request

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Request.Name: RoutineControl.Magnetic tone wheel control.RR status request
RoutineControl.Magnetic tone wheel control.RL status request
RoutineControl.Magnetic tone wheel control.FR status request
RoutineControl.Magnetic tone wheel control.FL status request

Request.Comment: You should remove ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.xxx data. These are examples.

SWAS
Device.Mnemonic: DTC about Steering Wheel Angle Sensor

Label.Text: A reset of the totalizer odometer is only allowed after a driving test in the following situation:
- Totalizer odometer has not been reset more than 2 times
Renault Spare Part Number (Ref B for plant)
Vehicle Manufacturer ECU SW Number (RUC = Ref B)
System Supplier ECU SW Version Number
Manual start response upon start request
Manual status response upon stop request
Manual start response upon start request
Manual status response upon stop request
Manual start response upon start request
Manual status response upon start request
Press the gaz pedal and reach the engine RPM between 2000 rpm and 3000 rpm. Then, start the ASR control.
Manual start response upon start request
Manual start response upon start request

Display.DataName: Function status.ASR function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.ASR function.inhibition by switch status
Function status.AYC function.inhibition by diag status

Function status.HBA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.HSA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.EBP function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.RMF function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.TSF function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.HLA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.HBB function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.ACC function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.AEBS function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.EG function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.BLA function.inhibition by diag status
Function status.VESS function.inhibition by diag status

Label.Text: Hydraulic block secondary circuit bleeding procedure
-1- Place the bleeding tool on the brake liquid tank
-3- Make sure that all bleeding screws are closed
-2- Perform a manual bleeding on the four wheels without any ABS or ESC activation (primary bleeding)
-5- Click on button preliminary flush cycle and wait the end of the control (duration = 20 sec.)
-11- When no air is remaining, close the FR bleeding screw and then stop the FR bleeding procedure
-12- When FR bleeding is over, click on the button flush cycle and wait the end of the control (duration = 20 sec.)
-10- If air bubbles were still present when the procedure stopped, reactivate the FR bleeding procedure until no air is present (return to step 10)
-6- When flush cycle is over, activate the FL bleeding procedure and then open the FL bleeding screw (duration = 30 sec.)
-7- If air bubbles were still present when the procedure stopped, reactivate the FL bleeding procedure until no air is present (return to step 7)
-9- When FL bleeding is over, activate the FR bleeding procedure and then open the FR bleeding screw (duration = 30 sec.)
-4- ALL ALONG the procedure (from step 5 to step 15), the brake pedal must be pressed and released alternately (approx. 3 sec. pressed / 3 sec. released)
-14- Perform a manual bleeding on the RR bleeding screw (primary bleeding on FL/RR axis)
-13- Perform a manual bleeding on the RL bleeding screw (primary bleeding on FR/RL axis)
-15- You can release the brake pedal and stop and disconnect the bleeding tool
-8- When no air is remaining, close the FL bleeding screw and stop the FL bleeding procedure

Label.Text: WARNING : Do not perform the hydraulic circuit control if the hydraulic circuit is not well bled
To check if the hydraulic circuit is well bled:
If the pedal is hard, you can perform the hydraulic circuit control
- Stop the engine and press 3 times the brake pedal:
If the pedal is soft : air is present in the primary hydraulic circuit ==> perform the hydraulic bleeding procedure
A - Keep pressed the brake pedal
C - Start the routine control by pushing the button of the approprate wheel (routine time = 4 sec)
Reading of the valves and pump status
B - Another personn have to try to manually turn the wheel to test
Remark : Routines have to be started one by one (simultaneous controls are forbidden)
D - During the routine, if the selected wheel is successively blocked/unblocked ==> Control is OK

Hydraulic valves and pump.FL RR Electric shuttle EV
Hydraulic valves and pump.FL RR Isolating EV
Hydraulic valves and pump.FR RL Electric shuttle EV
Hydraulic valves and pump.FR RL Isolating EV

Label.Text: 1- Select the wheel to check (the wheel speed sensor shall not be in failure)
96 poles are detected --> No tone failure
2- Turn the wheel selected to do exactly 1 wheel revolution without coming back in inverse rotation direction
Less than 96 poles (result is pair) --> Tone must be replaced
More than 96 poles --> The control routine has failed, try again from step 1
3- Stop the routine or select another wheel from step 1
Number of poles is impair --> Re perform the counting from step 1

Display.RequestName: RoutineControl.Magnetic tone wheel control.FL status request
RoutineControl.Magnetic tone wheel control.FL status request

Label.Text: Warning : This calibration routine shall not be used for the default vehicle configuration This service is only available for development needs.
Before executing the routine : the steering wheel angle shall be without failure and the steering shall be macanically ajusted right
Before executing the routine : the ECU shall be without failure and the vehicle shall be on a plane surface
Before executing the routine : the ECU shall be without failure and the brake pedal shall be released

Screen.Name: A - Hydraulic valves and pump control

Label.Text: Reading of the valves and pump status

Label.Text: IS gateway activation / deactivation (In Supplier session only $1070)
Manual request for tire real circumference

Click to configure ESC for 15-40 mule cars. Calibrate first ESC with the correct pdx file depending on engine on the mule car.
6. MVVS are not equipped with an analog vacuum sensor, so configure it as "Not Present"
Before configurating, place the steering wheel at 0°, Front wheels in straight line, car on a horizontal surface and release the brake pedal
1. Press this button to start some basic configurations - Skip this step if a calibration file has been downloaded!
7. Configure the functions in screen 11 ECU configuration. Be careful, TSA and ARP shall not be activated and on 4WS cars, ESC functions shall all be deactivated.
Click for specific configuration for mule cars
Click for specific configuration for mule cars
Click to configure ESC for 15-40 mule cars. Calibrate first ESC with the correct pdx file depending on engine on the mule car.
6. MVVS are not equipped with an analog vacuum sensor, so configure it as "Not Present"
Before configurating, place the steering wheel at 0°, Front wheels in straight line, car on a horizontal surface and release the brake pedal
1. Press this button to start some basic configurations - Skip this step if a calibration file has been downloaded!
7. Configure the functions in screen 11 ECU configuration. Be careful, TSA and ARP shall not be activated and on 4WS cars, ESC functions shall all be deactivated.
Click for specific configuration for mule cars
Click for specific configuration for mule cars
